title: Durable repository harness state
acceptance: A new agent session can find progress and feature state from repository files.
The bootstrap command runs the cheapest existing smoke test.

title: Citation and documentation-map guardrail
acceptance: Markdown harness sources reject citations outside [S1]-[S5].
Verification fails when a required harness artifact is missing.
evidence: 2026-05-25: node --test tests/verify-harness.test.mjs passed with 4 tests passing.
2026-05-25: npm run verify passed and reported Harness verification passed.

title: Project-local package skill installation
acceptance: Running the package initializer installs bundled skills under the target project's .agents/skills directory.
The initializer does not install bundled skills into the user's home .agents/skills directory.
Documentation describes the project-local installation path.
evidence: 2026-05-25: node --test tests/install-skills.test.mjs failed before the implementation change because project-local skill files were absent, then passed with 6 tests after the change.
2026-05-25: npm run verify passed with Harness verification passed and 10 tests passing.
2026-05-25: npm run pack:dry passed for codex-harness-engineering-0.1.3.tgz.

title: Durable-state update gate for behavior changes
acceptance: Verification fails when an open implementation or harness-behavior change omits feature_list.json or progress.md updates.
Verification fails when changed behavior artifacts are mentioned only in an older progress entry.
The agent contract requires state updates for small tasks that change behavior or guardrails.
evidence: 2026-05-25: regression tests failed before verifier changes because behavior diffs without current progress linkage were accepted.
2026-05-25: node --test tests/verify-harness.test.mjs passed with 7 tests after implementing the gate.
2026-05-25: npm run verify passed with Harness verification passed and 13 tests passing.
2026-05-25: npm run pack:dry passed for codex-harness-engineering-0.1.3.tgz.

title: Reusable harness standard for target projects
acceptance: The implementation playbook defines the minimum artifacts and state-update contract for a new target project.
The playbook defines a mechanical gate for behavior changes and explains how to choose the diff baseline.
The playbook provides a bootstrap checklist including a negative test for guardrail enforcement.
evidence: 2026-05-25: npm run verify passed with Harness verification passed and 13 tests passing.
2026-05-25: npm run pack:dry passed for codex-harness-engineering-0.1.3.tgz.
